item.title: DDA review is a right call says ACT NOW!
item.description: [Act Now PNG] There is clearly a huge transparency and accountability gap that still exists within DDAs. The call by the Constitutional Law Reform Commission to review DDA law is a right call.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: How a local fintech solved two ‘serious’ problems with land management
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Port Moresby firm NiuPay has found a way to turn Papua New Guinea's new strata title law into a working, digital reality. Its Digital Strata Title Platform was one of the winners at this year's Innovation PNG Awards, with founder James Inglis sharing how it works – and the problems it solves – in hi

item.title: Protecting Pacific Taro
item.description: [Education News PNG] USP Researcher Tackles Climate Challenges As the world marks World Rural Development Day on 6 July, attention turns to the vital role rural communities play The post Protecting Pacific Taro first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: PNG Leader Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific "Ocean of Peace" After Chinese Missile Test
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has urged major military powers across the globe to honour the Pacific as a zone of peace, after China fired a long-range ballistic missile into open waters in the South Pacific region as part of a test launch, directly affecting waters close to Papua New Guinea and its n

item.title: Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea faces significant gold smuggling issues, undermining its economy and reducing revenue for essential services. Efforts are being made to enhance monitoring and disrupt smuggling networks for better resource management. The post Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAW

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific Free From Military Weapons Testing
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has appealed to China and other military powers to stop using the Pacific Ocean for missile tests and weapons trials after a strategic ballistic missile was launched into international waters in the South Pacific. Describing the Pacific as a region that should be prot

item.title: PNG Power will appeal the Dirio Court Decision
item.description: [Post Courier] PNG Power Limited Chief Executive Officer Paul Bayly has responded to recent media coverage of the National Court judgement of July 7, 2026 regarding a claim by Dirio Gas and Power Limited against PPL, KCH and the State. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web

item.title: Australia launches K760 million Pacific Rugby League Partnership
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] AUSTRALIA has announced a new $250 million (K760m), 10-year Pacific Rugby League Partnership, aimed at strengthening rugby league across the Pacific while creating opportunities in education, leadership and employment. Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese joined Papua New Guinea Prime Minister

item.title: Australian Partnership helps PNG Cocoa farmers access premium markets
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Papua New Guinea cocoa farmers are receiving Australian support to meet international sustainability standards and secure access to premium overseas markets through a partnership with leading cocoa exporter NGIP-Agmark. The initiative, launched to coincide with World Chocolate Day, is helping farmer

item.title: Ol ami rausim planti bom long East New Britain provins
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol bom ekspet na sapot opisa bilong dispela wok i kam long Australia, PNG, New Zealand, Canada, France, Japan, UK na USA i bin stap insait long wanpela operesen we ol i rausim inap 2,200 bom bilong Wol Woa 2 long East New Britain provins long Jun dispela yia. Australia i go pas long dispela wok anin

item.title: Mining Project Sparks Fears of Environmental Disaster
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Major mining project that could discharge hundreds of millions of tonnes of waste into the sea off Papua New Guinea

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re always looking for opportunities’: Air Niugini’s new CEO on his first items of business
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Alan Milne was appointed to his second term as CEO of Air Niugini in early 2026, following a previous stint in 2018-2020. In this interview with Business Advantage PNG , Milne speaks about changing market conditions and plans for more planes and new routes, as well as a pending new corporate strateg

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: Newmont PNG expands trade training with national accreditation
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Newmont PNG has reached another milestone in workforce development after its Lihir Technical Training Centre secured national accreditation to conduct onsite Belt Splicing trade testing, giving more Papua New Guineans access to recognised trade qualifications without leaving the mine site. Newmont P

item.title: TVET and Universities in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's education system offers two main pathways: Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) focuses on practical skills for immediate employment, while universities emphasise theoretical knowledge and long-term career preparation. The post TVET and Universities in Papua New

item.title: PNG PM Marape Removes Minister Win Daki from Commerce Portfolio Pending SME Fund Probe
item.description: [PNG Facts] Prime Minister James Marape has stripped Minister Win Daki of the Commerce and Industry portfolio as the Government launches an independent investigation into allegations involving the administration of Papua New Guinea's Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Fund. The move also requires Commerce and In

item.title: Faith, family and grapes: Damien Paul’s vineyard dream grows in the Highlands
item.description: [Post Courier] From a broken vine to a booming vineyard, Damien Paul says faith and family turned his small garden into 2.7 hectares of grapes, one year after Post-Courier Online reported he had 1.7 hectares. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three-

item.title: GoPNG and QUT sign strategic partnership to accelerate national digital transformation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Government of Papua New Guinea (GoPNG) today signed a landmark Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Queensland University of Technology (QUT), establishing a strategic partnership to support Papua New Guinea’s National Digital Transformation Agenda. The MoU was signed by the Acting Ministe

item.title: PNG congratulates Solomon Islands on 48th Independence
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has congratulated the Government and people of the Solomon Islands on the country’s 48th Independence Anniversary, reaffirming Papua New Guinea’s commitment to strengthening its longstanding Melanesian partnership with its Pacific neighbor. In a statement marking the

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.description: [Act Now PNG] Constitutional Law Reform Commission (CLRC) will review the laws on DDA

Trump, his administration and the American people following celebrations marking 250 years of American independence on July 4. The Prime Minister described the milestone as a significant mo

item.title: Australia and Papua New Guinea formally become allies as Pukpuk Treaty takes effect
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] AUSTRALIA and Papua New Guinea have officially entered a new era of defence cooperation after the Pukpuk Treaty came into force, making the two neighbouring countries formal allies. Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape announced the treaty’s com

item.title: Solomon Airlines resumes Port Moresby-Honiara flights after 15-year break
item.description: [The PNG Sun] As Solomon Islands is celebrating its 48th Independence anniversary, the Solomon Airlines has resumed direct flights between Port Moresby and Honiara after a 15-year hiatus. This direct flight has restored weekly air services aimed at boosting tourism, trade, business, people-to-people and regional

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

item.title: TSC advertises 42, 000 teaching positions for 2027 school year
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Teaching Service Commission (TSC) has advertised 42,000 teaching positions nationwide for the 2027 academic year and directed eligible teachers and graduating education students to submit applications by July 31. Acting TSC Chairman Maini Mike Ugaia announced yesterday in a statement that the va

item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. item.title: Wapenamanda DDA pays K131, 000 in School Fee support for POM Business College students
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Wapenamanda District Development Authority (DDA) has paid K131,000 in school fee subsidies for Wapenamanda students attending Port Moresby Business College. This reaffirmed the DDA’s commitment to education as a key priority under the Kaeok Administration. Related news: https://thepngbulletin.co

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘The skills that matter are changing fast’: Q&A with Ben Hamer, keynote speaker at 2026 PNG Investment Conference
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Dr Ben Hamer, an international expert on the future of work, shares his views on which skills will be most in demand in the economies of the future – and where Papua New Guinea fits into the changing nature of work. item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG braces for an extraordinary dry season with looming water shortages and crop stress. Districts ordered to map local water sources. item.title: SP PNG Hunters at the Crossroads as Last-Place Finish Raises Tough Questions Over Direction
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Commentary by George Lemko The PNG SP Hunters have reached a point where difficult questions can no longer be ignored. The latest Hostplus Cup ladder paints a worrying picture, with the Hunters sitting at the bottom of the 15-team competition after another disappointing campaign. While rival clubs c

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM Marape Confirms Suwae as Acting PNG Chief Ombudsman
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG PM Marape Confirms Suwae as Acting PNG Chief Ombudsman PNG Prime Minister James Marape has confirmed the appointment of Tabitha Suwae as Acting Chief Ombudsman following the completion of Richard Pagen's six-year term, with the Government beginning the process of recruiting a permanent head for

item.description: [Post Courier] Twenty-six foreign nationals have been taken into custody after a joint police and immigration operation in Morobe. item.title: National court rules in favour of Dirio Power in dispute with PNG Power
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] DIRIO Power has welcomed a National Court ruling in its favour, bringing to a close legal proceedings over what the company said was PNG Power’s failure to meet its payment obligations under the two companies’ Power Purchase Agreement (PPA). The company said the judgment affirmed its long-held posit

item.title: New Ireland government commits K100, 000 to AFL PNG Youth Development
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The New Ireland Provincial Government has committed K100,000 to AFL Papua New Guinea to expand grassroots football programs and create more opportunities for young people through sport across the province. Governor Byron Chan announced the funding partnership at Colts Oval in Port Moresby this week,

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Iconic brewer looking to make a social impact in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] SP Brewery’s beers have been sold in PNG since 1952, almost a quarter-century before independence. Now the company is looking to invest in social change alongside business growth opportunities, its Managing Director Ed Weggemans tells Business Advantage PNG . item.description: [Inside PNG] NARI urges PNG to prioritize breadfruit as a key food security crop to protect local communities from severe El Niño droughts. item.title: Two young men charged after taxi driver kidnapped and left in bushes
item.description: [Post Courier] Two young men have been charged after a taxi driver was allegedly kidnapped, robbed and left tied up in bushes for almost 12 hours along the Madang-Ramu Highway. item.title: PEBs warn of teacher appointment delays
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By DALCY LULUA TEACHING Service Commission (TSC) Acting Chairman Maini Mike Ugaia has warned provincial governments to ensure their Provincial Education Boards (PEBs) are fully constituted and operational by the end of July or risk losing the authority to appoint teachers for the 2027 academic year.

item.title: PNG’s Economic Growth stays strong: Westpac reports
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Westpac’s latest PNG Economic Update and Outlook says PNG’s growth outlook remains strong, supported by ongoing activity in agriculture, industry and services. The report forecasts real GDP growth of around 4.6 per cent in 2026, following estimated growth of 5.5 per cent in 2025. Related news: https

item.title: More complaints of illegal FCA logging
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities in remote Nakanai region of West New Britain say their forests are being illegally destroyed by a Malaysian logging company

item.title: PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nine Chinese nationals arrested in PNG's biggest gold-smuggling bust. 14.5kg of gold sand found on flight PX8. Strict bail granted. item.title: PNG Defence Force Personnel Complete Trade Training Under Australia Partnership
item.description: [PNG Facts] Thirty-five members of the PNG Defence Force (PNGDF) have graduated from an eight-week vocational training program, equipping them with practical automotive and carpentry skills to strengthen the organisation's technical workforce. item.title: Cocoa boost local households in PNG
item.description: [Post Courier] In the first half of 2026 strong earnings from Cocoa have supported rural households in the country with buying price for dried Cocoa beans generally ranging from K700 to K800 per bag. item.title: Health and education policies made easier to understand
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] GOVERNMENT agencies, church partners, and other stakeholders have endorsed a series of easy-to-understand booklets aimed at helping people better understand government health and education policies. Developed with support from the PNGAus Partnership, the booklets explain the health and education ser

item.title: PNG’s Forex flow has improved: Westpac report says
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Foreign exchange liquidity in PNG has improved significantly, helping businesses and supporting private sector lending, according to the Westpac’s latest PNG Economic Update and Outlook. The report says IMF-led reforms and stronger resource export earnings have helped reduce PNG’s long-running forei

item.title: PNG Democracy Can Recover Under New Electoral Commissioner, Says Ipatas
item.description: [PNG Facts] Enga Governor Chief Sir Peter Ipatas Enga Governor Chief Sir Peter Ipatas says Papua New Guinea has endured too many disappointing elections, but believes the country has an opportunity to restore public confidence following the appointment of Dr Nicole Haley as Electoral Commissioner. Throwing his

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: PNG Trade Union Calls for Clarity on Foreign Appointments
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNGTUC Assistant Secretary General, Anton Sekum The PNG Trade Union Congress is calling for greater transparency over the appointment of foreign nationals to senior public service positions, saying the government should clearly explain the roles, responsibilities and terms of engagement for expatria
